content.problemStatement:
There is a full-scale war taking place in Treeland. Chef needs to travel between cities in Treeland to work, but during the war, it is not safe.

In Treeland, there are NNN cities (numbered 111 through NNN) and N−1N - 1N−1 bidirectional roads connecting them in such a way that Chef can reach each city from any other city using these roads. Each road is occupied by a unique faction that assigned a positive integer code to it.

Let f(A,B)f(A, B)f(A,B) denote the greatest positive integer which divides the code of each road on the shortest path between cities AAA and BBB (the GCD of these codes). If Chef wants to go from city AAA to city BBB safely, he needs to pay f(A,B)f(A, B)f(A,B) units of money.

Since Chef is not sure between which pairs of cities he is going to travel, he wants to know
S=∑i=1N∑j=i+1Nf(i,j) .S = \sum\limits_{i=1}^{N} \sum\limits_{j=i+1}^{N} f(i, j) \,.S=i=1∑N​j=i+1∑N​f(i,j).

Help Chef calculate SSS.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
Each of the next N−1N-1N−1 lines contains three space-separated integers XXX, YYY and ZZZ denoting that cities XXX and YYY are connected by a road with code ZZZ.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer SSS.
